title: Anthropic Introduces Claude Opus 4 and Sonnet 4, Setting New Records for Coding and Reasoning
summary: Anthropic launches Claude Opus 4 and Sonnet 4, with Opus 4 claiming the top spot on SWE-bench at 72.5% and the ability to run agent tasks continuously for several hours.
content: Anthropic has introduced Claude Opus 4 and Claude Sonnet 4, its next-generation AI models setting new standards for coding, advanced reasoning, and long-horizon agent workflows. Claude Opus 4 is described as the world's best coding model, achieving 72.5% on SWE-bench Verified and demonstrating the ability to work continuously for several hours — handling thousands of sequential steps on complex tasks. Alongside the new models, Anthropic announced extended thinking with tool use, parallel tool execution, and significantly improved memory capabilities that let agents extract and store key facts across sessions, enabling coherence over long projects. Claude Code is now generally available with native VS Code and JetBrains integrations, and a new Claude Code SDK lets developers build custom agents on the same foundation — including a beta Claude Code GitHub app for responding to PR feedback and CI errors. Both models are available on Anthropic's API, Amazon Bedrock, and Google Cloud's Vertex AI, with Opus 4 priced at $15/$75 per million tokens and Sonnet 4 at $3/$15.

title: OpenAI Launches Codex, a Cloud-Based AI Coding Agent Inside ChatGPT
summary: OpenAI debuts Codex, powered by codex-1 (a variant of o3), that runs in a sandboxed cloud environment and can autonomously write features, fix bugs, and open pull requests in parallel.
content: OpenAI launched Codex, a cloud-based AI coding agent powered by codex-1 — a version of the o3 reasoning model optimized specifically for software engineering via reinforcement learning on real-world coding tasks. The agent runs in a sandboxed virtual environment preloaded with users' GitHub repositories and can write features, fix bugs, answer codebase questions, and propose pull requests — handling multiple tasks in parallel for anywhere from one to thirty minutes without interrupting the user. OpenAI says codex-1 produces cleaner code than o3, adheres more precisely to instructions, and iteratively runs tests until passing results are achieved before presenting its work. Codex is rolling out to ChatGPT Pro, Enterprise, and Business subscribers globally, operates in an air-gapped environment for security, and is also updating Codex CLI with a new codex-mini-latest model available in the API at $1.50 per million input tokens. OpenAI's agents research team says the long-term vision is for Codex to act as 'virtual teammates' capable of handling tasks that take human engineers hours or even days.

title: Google Unveils Gemini 2.5 Upgrades at I/O: Deep Think Mode, Native Audio, and MCP Support
summary: Google announced major Gemini 2.5 updates at I/O 2025, including a Deep Think enhanced reasoning mode, native audio output, thinking budgets for cost control, and built-in Model Context Protocol support.
content: At Google I/O 2025, Google DeepMind announced significant updates to its Gemini 2.5 model family, including a new experimental Deep Think enhanced reasoning mode for 2.5 Pro designed for highly complex math and coding challenges. Both 2.5 Pro and 2.5 Flash are gaining native audio output for more natural conversational experiences, with a new Live API supporting affective dialogue (detecting emotion in a user's voice), proactive audio (ignoring background conversations), and multi-speaker text-to-speech. Google extended thinking budget controls — previously Flash-only — to 2.5 Pro, letting developers dial the number of reasoning tokens up or down to balance latency and cost. Gemini 2.5 Flash is now available to all users in the Gemini app and will reach general availability in Google AI Studio and Vertex AI in early June; Google also added native MCP support to the Gemini API and SDK for easier integration with open-source tooling. Project Mariner's computer use capabilities are also being brought into the Gemini API and Vertex AI, opening browser automation to enterprise developers building agentic applications.

title: GitHub Copilot Gets a Full Autonomous Coding Agent That Works Like a Team Member
summary: GitHub launched a new coding agent for Copilot that accepts GitHub issue assignments, spins up a VM via GitHub Actions, and autonomously commits code to a draft PR — powered by Claude Sonnet 4.
content: GitHub has launched a new coding agent embedded directly into its platform, allowing developers to assign GitHub issues to Copilot the same way they would assign work to a human teammate — via github.com, GitHub Mobile, or the CLI. Once assigned, the agent spins up a secure virtual machine on GitHub Actions, clones the repository, analyzes the codebase using RAG-powered code search, and iteratively pushes commits to a draft pull request while logging its full reasoning in session logs for transparency. The agent supports Model Context Protocol for pulling in context from outside GitHub, can interpret images in issues such as bug screenshots or UI mockups, and follows custom repository instructions to match coding standards. Security is enforced by design: the agent can only push to branches it creates, human approval is required before any CI/CD pipeline runs, and the developer who assigned the task cannot be the one to approve the resulting pull request. The new coding agent — powered by Anthropic's Claude Sonnet 4 — is available now to Copilot Enterprise and Copilot Pro+ subscribers.

title: Google Rolls Out AI Mode in Search to All US Users, Signaling the End of the Blue Link Era
summary: Google expanded its conversational AI Mode to all US users at I/O 2025, adding Deep Search, Project Mariner agentic browsing, Search Live with camera input, and personalized context from email and search history.
content: Google announced at I/O 2025 that it is rolling AI Mode out to all US Google users without a waitlist, making its Gemini-powered conversational search experience a default option in Google Search for the first time. The expanded AI Mode introduces Deep Search — Google's take on the deep research trend — which synthesizes information across many sources into broad, coherent summaries, plus Project Mariner integration that can browse the web and complete tasks like purchasing tickets or finding the best prices entirely on users' behalf. AI Mode can now access users' historical search queries and, optionally, email inboxes to give Gemini rich personal context, while a new Search Live feature enables real-time queries using voice conversation and live camera input. Google reported that 1.5 billion people already see AI Overviews every month, with click-through rates to some sources declining as users find sufficient answers directly in the summaries — a structural shift that Google's search chief Liz Reid says is only accelerating. Google executives framed AI Mode as the long-term future of Search, describing the traditional results page as a historical 'construct' that will be largely unrecognizable within just a few years.

title: Microsoft Build 2025: Copilot Tuning and Multi-Agent Orchestration Arrive for Enterprises
summary: Microsoft announced Copilot Tuning for low-code model customization on company data and multi-agent orchestration so specialized AI agents can collaborate on complex enterprise workflows.
content: At Microsoft Build 2025, Microsoft announced Copilot Tuning — a low-code capability in Copilot Studio that lets organizations fine-tune AI models on their own company data, workflows, and institutional knowledge without needing data scientists or weeks of effort. Paired with new multi-agent orchestration, businesses can now deploy teams of specialized agents that exchange data, collaborate on tasks, and divide work by expertise — for example, coordinating HR, IT, and marketing agents together to onboard a new employee. Microsoft also introduced Azure AI Foundry integration giving Copilot Studio access to more than 1,900 models, while Microsoft Entra Agent ID automatically assigns a managed identity to every agent for enterprise-grade security governance and compliance. These announcements build on momentum from over 230,000 organizations already using Copilot Studio, including 90% of the Fortune 500, with more than 1 million custom agents created in the last quarter alone — a 130% quarter-over-quarter increase. Copilot Wave 2's spring release, including Copilot Notebooks and new Researcher and Analyst reasoning agents, also moved to general availability at the event.
